ABOUT THIS SET

A working set of technical drawings, models, and finishes documentation.

This portfolio brings together three drawing sheets produced using AutoCAD — spanning 3D massing studies, interior fit-out and finishes planning, and fully annotated building elevations. Each sheet below is presented as it was submitted, with a short note on what it shows and the drafting skills it demonstrates.

Original A3 drawing sheets, full resolution — download below or scroll for individual sheets
ICE TASK 3.PDF DRAWING 4.PDF FORMATIVE 2.PDF
SHEET 01 / 03  —  DWG NO. ICE-T3
3D Modeling Exercise — ICE Task 3
3D MODELING ELEVATION STUDY
SUMMARY

A digitally produced massing study showing the entrance volume of a building in elevation, oriented with a north arrow and set against dashed construction guidelines that trace the rest of the structure beyond. The grey blockwork distinguishes separate building volumes and openings, while thin projection lines tie the model back to the site boundary.

The exercise demonstrates the ability to translate a 2D drawing into a coordinated 3D digital model — reading proportions, working out volumes, and presenting them cleanly on a standard A3 layout sheet.

TASK

SHEET 02 / 03  —  DWG NO. POE-04
Interior Fit-Out & Finishes Plan
FLOOR PLAN FINISHES SCHEDULE
SUMMARY

A detailed full-floor layout plan documenting room finishes and built-in elements across an open-plan living, kitchen, and bedroom layout. Flooring types — timber, carpet, and ceramic tile — are called out room by room with their respective areas, alongside built-in cupboards, sanitaryware (WHB, WC, shower), and a double-volume space marked with a 1100mm-high glass balustrade.

Riser and tread dimensions are noted for the staircase, downpipe and sewer-pipe routing is shown with fall directions, and feature elements such as a fireplace, TV feature wall, and roof garden planters are positioned and labelled, reflecting a complete interior documentation set ready for construction reference.

SHEET 03 / 03  —  DWG NO. FORM-02
Building Elevations — Formative 2
ELEVATIONS CONSTRUCTION DETAIL
SUMMARY

A fully specified set of building elevations covering the front and side faces of a residence, drawn at a consistent set of floor and roof datum levels — from natural ground level up to top-of-roof. Each elevation carries its own dimension string and level markers (FFL, top-of-wallplate, underside-of-lintol), giving a clear vertical reading of the building.

Construction notes describe the Klip-Lok 770 roof sheeting profile, its supporting timber truss and wallplate fixing method, and the 30° roof pitch, while window and door tags (W/WT, D/DT), a stone-chip concrete feature slab, glass balustrades, and uPVC sewer pipe falls round out the technical detailing. This sheet reflects the formative assessment standard for combining structural, roofing, and finishing information on one drawing.

ASSESSMENT
Formative 2
